game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Long Neck Run:** Players control a character in an endless runner, automatically moving forward on a single lane, using left/right swipes to navigate horizontally. The core mechanic revolves around the character's neck length, which starts at 5 segments; collecting rings matching the character's current color adds 1 segment, while hitting a wrong-colored ring removes 2 segments. Obstacles like low barriers or zip lines require a minimum neck height to pass, otherwise, the character loses 5 neck segments or the run ends if the neck is too short. Difficulty scales as the procedurally generated track increases in complexity and the character's speed incrementally rises by 5% every 15 seconds. The run concludes if the neck length reaches zero or the character falls off the track, with the primary goal being to achieve the highest distance and neck length to earn keys and gemstones for unlocking cosmetic skins.
game_info.description: Navigate an endless runner as a character with a growing neck! Collect rings matching your current color to extend your neck, avoid wrong-colored rings that shrink it, and duck under or jump over obstacles. Your neck must be long enough to clear barriers and ziplines. The track gets faster and more complex over time. Survive as long as possible and achieve the maximum distance!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left: Move character left
Arrow Right: Move character right
Arrow Up: Jump (when on ground)
Arrow Down: Duck (crouch to pass under low obstacles)
ENTER: Start game (from start screen)
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (return to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and ring collection test. Validates that the character can move horizontally, collect rings of matching colors, and that neck length increases correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the character left and right to collect rings while avoiding wrong-colored ones. Prioritize staying alive and collecting matching rings to grow the neck.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Character successfully moves across lanes, collects matching rings, neck length increases appropriately, and the game continues without crashing. Test is successful if it runs for at least 500 frames without game over.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test - achieving maximum distance and neck length. Tests the ability to navigate complex obstacles, manage speed increases, and maximize survival distance.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use optimal pathfinding to collect matching rings, avoid wrong-colored rings, duck under low barriers, and ensure neck is long enough for ziplines. Adaptively respond to increasing speed and complexity.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Character survives for an extended period (1500+ frames), achieves high distance score (2000+), maintains healthy neck length (10+ segments), and demonstrates ability to handle all obstacle types successfully.

TEST_2.expected: Character survives for an extended period (1500+ frames), achieves high distance score (2000+), maintains healthy neck length (10+ segments), and demonstrates ability to handle all obstacle types successfully.

TEST_3.test_description: Obstacle interaction test. Validates collision detection with barriers, ziplines, and wrong-colored rings, ensuring proper neck segment penalties are applied.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Intentionally interact with various obstacles to test collision mechanics - hit wrong rings to test segment loss, attempt barriers with insufficient neck length, and test ducking mechanics.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All collision types work correctly: wrong rings remove 2 segments, barriers require minimum neck height or remove 5 segments, and ducking allows passing under low obstacles. Test validates game over when neck reaches zero.
